description: Plan readability + self-review guidance injected on plan-mode entry. Self-gates on tool_name=EnterPlanMode so it stays silent for the other PreToolUse tools the engine now runs on (e.g. Bash). The EnterPlanMode context persists for the whole plan-mode session, so it covers both initial creation and every revision - the right timing for a pre-presentation review (an ExitPlanMode nudge would land too late).

text: A plan is easier to act on - and keeps both the reviewer and the context lean - when it reads as the current state of intent rather than its history.

So keep the problem statement but drop the decision history (rejected approaches, revision rationale, prior iterations), and on a revision rewrite the whole plan clean rather than annotating what changed.
Anchor each step to a real file path (e.g., src/auth.ts:42) and favor a terse action over explanatory prose.
Before presenting, re-read the plan for flaws that look fine on the first pass: assumptions not checked against the code, steps that contradict each other, drift from what was asked. Fix them first.
